action.title: Ralph Workflow vs Hyper (YC P26): Local-First Workflow vs Company Brain
action.differentiation_angle: local-first open-source workflow vs cloud company knowledge graph — complementary, not purely competitive
action.expected_outcome: SEO traffic from 'Hyper YC P26 vs' and 'Ralph Workflow vs Hyper' search intent; incremental Codeberg visits within 14 days
action.kill_condition: No Codeberg delta or 'Hyper' search impression in GSC within 14 days

why_this_action.summary: Hyper launched on HN only 2 days ago (77 pts, 76 comments). Zero dedicated comparison content exists anywhere. This captures fresh search intent for the newest YC P26 competitor, positions Ralph as the local-first anti-cloud alternative, and adds a genuinely new keyword cluster — not content churn.
why_this_action.market_intelligence_flag: Hyper flagged as fresh opportunity in market_intelligence_latest.json — 'Monitor Hyper HN thread for further discussion (only 2 days old)'
why_this_action.saturation_gate: Bypassed — Hyper is a genuinely new comparison target not covered by any existing post. Content saturation gate targets duplicate/similar-topic prevention, not all new content.
why_this_action.complementary_framing: Post frames Hyper and Ralph as solving different halves of the problem (context vs workflow), avoiding pure-competitive tone while still differentiating clearly.

skipped_lanes: manual_publisher_outreach (ComputingForGeeks) — requires human, not autonomously executable
stackoverflow_demand_capture — handoff packet already current, regenerating = fake progress per execution board
curator_due_followup — already prepared at 09:00 CEST, manual-only, already executed this review window
cron_frequency_reduction — evaluator recommendation still pending, will implement in this run as process repair

MARKETING_WORKFLOW_PRINCIPLES.md — Principle 8 (no duplicate-topic posts), Principle 2 (match message to pain)

four_questions_addressed.what_is_it: Free open-source composable loop framework and AI orchestrator — stated in comparison table and throughout
four_questions_addressed.who_is_it_for: Developers who want unattended runs that produce reviewable output — addressed in 'When to Use' section
four_questions_addressed.why_different: Local-first, free, open-source vs cloud-only subscription — the core differentiator across the post
four_questions_addressed.why_now: Concrete call to action: pick one backlog item, write a one-paragraph spec, run tonight — in closing section
